archetype: Automated Satellite Constellation Configuration and Collision Avoidance System for Low Earth Orbit (LEO) Satellite Networks
domain: Satellite Operations and Space Situational Awareness
description: This system automates the configuration and management of satellite constellations in Low Earth Orbit (LEO), predicting and preventing potential collisions with other satellites and space debris. It is used by satellite operators and space agencies to ensure safe and efficient operation of their satellite fleets. The system must integrate with various data sources, such as satellite tracking systems and weather forecasts, and adhere to international regulations and standards for space situational awareness.
rules: MUST integrate with the Space Data Association (SDA) format for satellite tracking data
MUST comply with the International Organization for Standardization (ISO) 24113 standard for space debris mitigation
MUST incorporate weather forecast data from the National Weather Service (NWS) or equivalent
MUST adhere to the United Nations Committee on the Peaceful Uses of Outer Space (COPUOS) guidelines for responsible satellite operations
MUST support multiple satellite bus architectures and propulsion systems
anti_patterns: DO NOT use a simplified, two-dimensional collision detection algorithm, as it will not account for the complex orbital dynamics of LEO satellites
DO NOT rely solely on historical data for collision prediction, as it will not capture the rapidly changing environment of LEO
DO NOT neglect to account for the effects of atmospheric drag on satellite orbits
key_technical_challenges: Developing an efficient and accurate algorithm for collision detection and prediction in a highly dynamic and complex environment
Integrating and processing large amounts of data from various sources, including satellite tracking systems, weather forecasts, and space debris catalogs
Ensuring the system can scale to support large satellite constellations and handle high volumes of data in real-time

recommended_stack.language: Python, due to its extensive libraries and frameworks for scientific computing and data analysis
recommended_stack.framework: Django, for its robustness and scalability in handling complex data models and workflows
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL, for its support for spatial data types and efficient querying of large datasets
recommended_stack.cache: Redis, for its high-performance caching and ability to handle large amounts of data
recommended_stack.message_broker: Apache Kafka, for its scalability and fault-tolerance in handling high-volume data streams
